task_completion.description: Verify the 'alpha-backend-build' Pipeline job is created and configured with the correct Git repository and branch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins job configuration screen for 'alpha-backend-build' showing the 'Pipeline' section with the Git repository URL set to 'https://github.com/jenkinsci/pipeline-examples' and the branch specifier set to 'master' (or '*/master').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'alpha-backend-build' job is configured to poll SCM every 15 minutes using hash-based scheduling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins job configuration screen for 'alpha-backend-build' showing 'Poll SCM' checked under Build Triggers, with the schedule text box containing 'H/15' (e.g., 'H/15 * * * *').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'alpha-frontend-build' job has the correct NODE_VERSION choice parameter and build discarder settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins job configuration screen for 'alpha-frontend-build' showing 'This project is parameterized' checked with a Choice Parameter named 'NODE_VERSION' containing choices '16', '18', '20' on separate lines, AND 'Discard old builds' checked with 'Max # of builds to keep' set to '7'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'npm-registry-token' secret text credential was provisioned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins Credentials screen (Global credentials) showing a credential of kind 'Secret text' with the ID or Name 'npm-registry-token'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Project-Alpha CI' list view exists and contains both jobs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins dashboard showing a tab or view named 'Project-Alpha CI' selected, with both 'alpha-backend-build' and 'alpha-frontend-build' visible in the jobs list table.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Jenkins web UI to configure the environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Jenkins web interface in a browser, filling out forms, and clicking buttons, rather than just executing Groovy scripts or CLI commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually saved the configurations and didn't just type them into the forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the saved job pages, the credentials list, and the dashboard view reflecting the created items, not just unsubmitted configuration forms.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write the expected answers into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration values (URLs, cron patterns, parameters) must be entered into the actual Jenkins UI input fields, not a generic text editor or console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Backend Job: Name must be 'alpha-backend-build', Git URL must be 'https://github.com/jenkinsci/pipeline-examples', branch must be 'master', and Poll SCM schedule must include 'H/15' (e.g., 'H/15 * * * *'). Frontend Job: Name must be 'alpha-frontend-build', must have a Choice Parameter named 'NODE_VERSION' with options '16', '18', '20' (in that exact order), and a build discarder keeping '7' builds. Credential: Must be a 'Secret text' credential with ID 'npm-registry-token'. View: Must be a List View named 'Project-Alpha CI' containing both jobs.
